text.title: pnpm Antipatterns
text.summary: Common mistakes: mixing package managers, missing lockfile, hoisting, phantom deps, and lockfile churn.

antipattern.description: Running `npm install` or `yarn add` in a pnpm repo, creating a `package-lock.json` or `yarn.lock` alongside pnpm-lock.yaml.
antipattern.reason: Mixed lockfiles cause non-deterministic installs and CI failures. Enforce with `preinstall: "npx only-allow pnpm"` and add `package-lock.json` / `yarn.lock` to `.gitignore`.
Without a committed lockfile, CI installs different versions from development, causing "works on my machine" bugs. Always commit the lockfile.

antipattern.description: Setting `shamefully-hoist=true` to fix a "module not found" error.
antipattern.reason: Hoisting hides phantom dependency bugs and makes the project behave like npm. Fix phantom deps by adding the missing dep explicitly with `pnpm add`.

antipattern.description: Editing package.json dependencies by hand instead of using `pnpm add` / `pnpm remove`.
antipattern.reason: Manual edits bypass peer-dependency resolution, producing an inconsistent lockfile. Always use pnpm CLI commands.

antipattern.description: Using `pnpm audit` in a retry loop when no fix exists for a transitive CVE.
antipattern.reason: `pnpm audit` exits non-zero even for CVEs with no fix. Use `pnpm audit --prod` to skip dev deps; check reachability with `pnpm why`; pin via `.pnpmfile.cjs` if truly reachable and unfixed.

antipattern.description: Using `pnpm add x` at workspace root without `--filter` intending to add to a sub-package.
antipattern.reason: Without --filter, the dep installs at the root, not the intended package. Always scope with `--filter <pkg-name>`.
